title: Aloo Gobi

seo.title: How to Make Aloo Gobi — Easy Pakistani Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make authentic Aloo Gobi. This easy Pakistani recipe includes step-by-step instructions, tips, and serving ideas.
h2Headings: Pro Tips for the Best Results
seo.imageCaption: Delicious homemade Aloo Gobi — a beloved Pakistani classic

description: Aloo Gobi is a classic Pakistani vegetarian dish featuring potatoes and cauliflower cooked with aromatic spices. This hearty and flavorful curry is a staple in many Pakistani homes, offering a comforting and wholesome meal. It is perfect served with roti, naan, or rice for a truly authentic experience.

instructions.title: Prepare Vegetables
instructions.text: Heat oil in a large pot or karahi over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and fry until translucent and lightly golden, about 5-7 minutes.

instructions.title: Sauté Aromatics
instructions.text: Add the ginger-garlic paste and green chilies. Sauté for another minute until fragrant, being careful not to burn the garlic.

instructions.title: Add Spices and Tomatoes
instructions.text: Stir in the pureed tomatoes, turmeric powder, red chili powder, coriander powder, cumin powder, and salt. Cook the masala on medium-low heat, stirring occasionally, until the oil separates from the mixture (bhunai), which takes about 8-10 minutes.

instructions.title: Incorporate Potatoes and Cauliflower
instructions.text: Add the cubed potatoes to the masala and mix well. Cook for 5 minutes, then add the cauliflower florets. Gently mix everything together, ensuring the vegetables are coated with the spice mixture.

instructions.title: Simmer to Perfection
instructions.text: Add about half a cup of water, cover the pot, and reduce the heat to low. Let it simmer for 15-20 minutes, or until the potatoes and cauliflower are tender. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking and check for doneness.

instructions.title: Finishing Touches
instructions.text: Once the vegetables are tender, uncover the pot and increase heat to medium-high for a few minutes if there is excess water, allowing it to evaporate. Sprinkle with garam masala and fresh chopped coriander.

instructions.title: Serve Hot
instructions.text: Give it a final gentle stir. Serve the Aloo Gobi hot with fresh roti, naan, paratha, or steamed rice for a complete meal.

tips: For best results, cook Aloo Gobi on low heat after adding vegetables to allow them to cook in their own steam and absorb the flavors fully, rather than boiling them.
To prevent cauliflower from becoming mushy, add it a few minutes after the potatoes, as potatoes take longer to cook. Alternatively, parboil cauliflower briefly before adding.
Always adjust the level of green chilies and red chili powder according to your spice preference. A sprinkle of dried fenugreek leaves (kasuri methi) at the end adds a wonderful aroma.
article: Aloo Gobi is a beloved Pakistani Vegetarian dish enjoyed across Pakistan. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
